title: Roundly Spiced Ethiopian Corn Flour Dabo
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish made with corn flour, perfect for breakfast or lunch. This recipe is halal-friendly and features a unique jelly-like texture.
instructions: Combine corn flour and water in a large bowl, stirring until smooth.
Heat ghee in a ti-leaf wrap over medium heat, then add onion and garlic and cook until softened.
Add spices and salt to the onion mixture and stir to combine.
Pour the corn flour mixture into the ti-leaf wrap and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture starts to set.
Reduce heat to low and cook for 5-7 minutes, until the dabo has a glassy-jelly texture.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot.
Katsuramuki-cut the dabo into small pieces and serve with your favorite Ethiopian sides.
